7:["$","article",null,{"className":"prose prose-zinc max-w-none","children":[["$","header",null,{"className":"mb-8 not-prose","children":[["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-3xl font-bold mb-2","children":"Tensor Logic | Neural Architecture"}],["$","time",null,{"className":"text-sm text-muted-foreground","children":"October 5, 2025"}]]}],[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-semibold mb-4 mt-6","children":"The Gap Between Logic and Learning"}],"\n",["$","p",null,{"className":"mb-4 leading-relaxed","children":"Classical logic is symbolic: propositions are discrete, rules are exact, and deduction is certain. Neural networks are sub-symbolic: representations are distributed, inference is approximate, and learning is statistical."}],"\n",["$","p",null,{"className":"mb-4 leading-relaxed","children":"Can we bridge this gap?"}],"\n",["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-semibold mb-4 mt-6","children":"Tensor Logic"}],"\n",["$","p",null,{"className":"mb-4 leading-relaxed","children":"Tensor logic 17:["$","li",null,{"className":"mb-1","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Logical NOT"}],": ",["$","span",null,{"className":"katex","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"katex-mathml","children":["$","math",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML","children":["$","semantics",null,{"children":[["$","mrow",null,{"children":[["$","mi",null,{"mathvariant":"normal","children":"¬"}],["$","mi",null,{"children":"x"}],["$","mo",null,{"children":"="}],["$","mn",null,{"children":"1"}],["$","mo",null,{"children":"−"}],["$","mi",null,{"children":"x"}]]}],["$","annotation",null,{"encoding":"application/x-tex","children":"\\neg x = 1 - x"}]]}]}]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"katex-html","aria-hidden":"true","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"base","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"strut","style":{"height":"0.4306em"}}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mord","children":"¬"}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mord mathnormal","children":"x"}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mspace","style":{"marginRight":"0.2778em"}}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mrel","children":"="}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mspace","style":{"marginRight":"0.2778em"}}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"base","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"strut","style":{"height":"0.7278em","verticalAlign":"-0.0833em"}}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mord","children":"1"}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mspace","style":{"marginRight":"0.2222em"}}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mbin","children":"−"}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mspace","style":{"marginRight":"0.2222em"}}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"base","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"strut","style":{"height":"0.4306em"}}],["$","span",null,{"className":"mord mathnormal","children":"x"}]]}]]}]]}]]}]
18:["$","p",null,{"className":"mb-4 leading-relaxed","children":"These operations are differentiable, allowing gradient-based learning of logical rules."}]
19:["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-semibold mb-4 mt-6","children":"Neural Architecture for Pattern Matching"}]
1a:["$","p",null,{"className":"mb-4 leading-relaxed","children":"Consider a pattern matching task: given a sequence, identify if it matches a regular expression."}]
1b:["$","p",null,{"className":"mb-4 leading-relaxed","children":"Traditional approaches:"}]
1c:["$","ol",null,{"className":"list-decimal pl-6 mb-4","children":["\n",["$","li",null,{"className":"mb-1","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"DFA compilation"}],": Fast but inflexible"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"className":"mb-1","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Neural sequence models"}],": Flexible but slow"]}],"\n"]}]
1d:["$","p",null,{"className":"mb-4 leading-relaxed","children":"Tensor logic offers a middle ground:"}]
1e:["$","pre",null,{"className":"bg-muted p-4 rounded-lg overflow-x-auto mb-4","children":["$","code",null,{"className":"bg-muted px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded text-sm font-mono","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-comment","children":"# Simplified tensor logic pattern matcher"}],"\n",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-keyword","children":"def"}]," ",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-title function_","children":"tensor_match"}],"(",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-params","children":"pattern, sequence"}],"):\n    ",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-comment","children":"# Pattern is a learned tensor representing the regex"}],"\n    ",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-comment","children":"# Sequence is embedded as token tensors"}],"\n\n    ",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-comment","children":"# State transitions are learned tensor operations"}],"\n    state = initial_state\n    ",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-keyword","children":"for"}]," token ",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-keyword","children":"in"}]," sequence:\n        state = transition(state, token, pattern)\n        state = normalize(state)  ",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-comment","children":"# Maintain probabilistic interpretation"}],"\n\n    ",["$","span",null,{"className":"hljs-keyword","children":"return"}]," accept(state)\n"]}]}]
1f:["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-semibold mb-4 mt-6","children":"Applications"}]
20:["$","ol",null,{"className":"list-decimal pl-6 mb-4","children":["\n",["$","li",null,{"className":"mb-1","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Learning regex from examples"}],": Instead of hand-writing patterns, learn them from data"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"className":"mb-1","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fuzzy pattern matching"}],": Handle noisy inputs gracefully"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"className":"mb-1","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Compositional generalization"}],": Combine learned patterns to handle novel situations"]}],"\n"]}]
21:["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-semibold mb-4 mt-6","children":"Limitations"}]
22:["$","ul",null,{"className":"list-disc pl-6 mb-4","children":["\n",["$","li",null,{"className":"mb-1","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Computational cost"}],": Tensor operations are more expensive than discrete DFA transitions"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"className":"mb-1","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Interpretability"}],": Learned patterns are harder to debug than hand-written regex"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"className":"mb-1","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Theoretical guarantees"}],": Unlike DFAs, learned matchers don't have formal correctness proofs"]}],"\n"]}]
23:["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-semibold mb-4 mt-6","children":"Future Directions"}]
24:["$","p",null,{"className":"mb-4 leading-relaxed","children":"The most promising direction is neuro-symbolic integration: use neural networks for perception and pattern learning, but compile to symbolic representations for verification and deployment."}]
25:["$","p",null,{"className":"mb-4 leading-relaxed","children":"This mirrors the brain's dual-process theory: fast, intuitive pattern matching (System 1) and slow, deliberate reasoning (System 2)."}]
c:[["$","meta","0",{"charSet":"utf-8"}],["$","meta","1",{"name":"viewport","content":"width=device-width, initial-scale=1"}]]
26:I[27201,["/_next/static/chunks/ff1a16fafef87110.js","/_next/static/chunks/d2be314c3ece3fbe.js"],"IconMark"]
a:null
